title: Rava Kesari

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Rava (Sooji / Semolina – fine grain): 1 cup (150 g)
Sugar: 1 – 1¼ cup (200–250 g, adjust to taste)
Milk (optional, for richness): ½ cup (100 ml)
Saffron strands: 12–15 (soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk)
Edible orange food color (or natural saffron infusion): a pinch

instruction.title: Preparation
steps: Warm water + milk mixture in a saucepan. Stir in saffron strands and keep it hot.
Heat 2 tbsp ghee in a heavy-bottom pan, roast cashews until golden, then raisins until they puff. Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Roasting the Rava
steps: In the same pan, add 2 tbsp ghee and roast the rava on medium-low flame.
Roast until it turns aromatic, pale golden, and free-flowing (about 6–8 minutes). Do not brown.

instruction.title: Cooking the Base
steps: Slowly pour the hot saffron water–milk mixture into the roasted rava, stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Cook on medium heat until rava absorbs liquid and swells.

instruction.title: Sweetening & Enriching
steps: Add sugar gradually, stirring constantly (the mixture will loosen and then thicken again).
Add remaining ghee in stages, mixing until the kesari turns glossy and leaves the sides of the pan.
Stir in cardamom powder, fried cashews, and raisins.

instruction.title: Plating Suggestion (Fine Dining Style)
steps: Plate as individual saffron domes on banana leaf or a marble plate.
Garnish with edible gold leaf or micro basil for a luxe touch.
Serve warm with filter coffee or as part of a South Indian thali.

chefTips: Glossy Finish: Add ghee in stages rather than all at once. This prevents greasiness and ensures silkiness.
Balanced Sweetness: Restaurants often use slightly less sugar than homemade versions to keep it refined.
Texture: Ensure the rava is roasted perfectly—no shortcuts. This prevents stickiness and ensures a melt-in-mouth feel.
Aromatic Layering: Saffron + cardamom create a luxurious aroma. A drop of rose water can be added for a subtle floral note.
Serving Style: Mold the kesari into small domes using a katori (bowl), garnish with 2 cashews on top, and brush lightly with melted ghee for shine.
